summary: postinstall drops and executes Windows.exe from unrelated GitHub account
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook `node install.js` that runs automatically on `npm install`. On Windows hosts (the script exits on non-Windows and when NODE_ENV=development to evade developer sandboxes), install.js invokes PowerShell with `-NoProfile -NonInteractive -WindowStyle Hidden -ExecutionPolicy Bypass` to download an opaque executable from `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cphc811-ui/d3d/main/npm-sc-legit.exe` — a personal GitHub account with no relation to the package's declared purpose — writes it to %TEMP%, and starts it with a hidden window. The combination of an unrelated-third-party mutable raw.githubusercontent.com source, hidden-window bypass execution, dev-environment gating, and no integrity check is an install-time remote-code-execution dropper against Windows installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
